name: LoopTrack
idea: LoopTrack is a returns and reverse-logistics management platform for small and mid-size e-commerce brands. It centralizes return requests, automates carrier label generation and refund approval rules, and turns every returned item into a tracked asset that is inspected, restocked, refurbished, or written off — so brands stop losing margin to a returns process handled over email and spreadsheets.
problem: E-commerce brands under ~$20M revenue handle product returns through shared inboxes, carrier portals, and spreadsheets. Nobody can see where a returned item physically is, refunds get issued before items are inspected, and resellable inventory sits in cardboard boxes for weeks. The result is double-digit margin leakage on every return cycle and support teams drowning in 'where is my refund?' tickets.
company_context: We are a three-person team that spun out of a 3PL warehouse operator. Two of us ran the returns dock for fashion and electronics clients for five years; the third built the warehouse's internal tooling. We watched dozens of brands lose money on returns because their 'process' was a shared inbox and a spreadsheet, and we are building LoopTrack as the system we always wished our clients had.
pain_points: Return requests arrive through email and marketplace portals and get tracked by hand in spreadsheets
Refunds are issued before the returned item is inspected, so damaged or wrong items still get refunded
Resellable returned inventory sits unprocessed for weeks and misses its sales window
No visibility into where a returned item physically is between the customer and the warehouse shelf

target_users.label: E-commerce Operations Managers
target_users.description: Ops leads at direct-to-consumer brands doing 500-5,000 orders/month who own the returns process end to end without dedicated tooling

target_users.label: 3PL Returns Supervisors
target_users.description: Warehouse-side supervisors who receive, inspect, and disposition returned items for multiple brand clients

objectives: Cut refund-before-inspection losses to near zero within the first quarter of adoption
Reduce average return-to-restock time from weeks to under 5 business days
Give brands a single tracked timeline for every returned item

audience.notes: Founders ran a 3PL returns dock for five years and speak with brand ops managers weekly.

competitive_landscape: Loop Returns and Happy Returns dominate the enterprise/Shopify-Plus tier; AfterShip Returns covers label generation but not warehouse-side disposition. Nobody serves the sub-$20M brand + independent 3PL pair as one shared workflow — that two-sided workflow is the wedge.
business_constraints: Bootstrapped; must integrate with Shopify and ShipStation at launch (no custom EDI). Two engineers; warehouse-side UI must work on rugged Android scanners. Target price point under $500/month for brands.

brand.logo_usage: Use the full triangular loop mark on light backgrounds for dashboard headers and reports; reserve the compact single-color variant for favicons, scanner-app icons, and shipping-label corners.
brand.brand_voice: Operational and direct — speaks like a seasoned warehouse lead: concrete numbers, no fluff, every claim tied to a step a user can act on.
brand.design_notes: Clean corporate-blue design system: high-contrast navy on light surfaces, generous whitespace, data-dense tables with clear status chips. The interface should feel like reliable logistics software, not a marketing site.
